children.heading: Relationship of Agreement to United States law
children.label: a Relationship of Agreement to United States law

children.heading: United States law to prevail in conflict
children.label: 1 United States law to prevail in conflict
children.text: No provision of the Agreement, nor the application of any such provision to any person or circumstance, which is inconsistent with any law of the United States shall have effect.

children.label: A
children.text: to amend or modify any law of the United States, or

children.label: B
children.text: to limit any authority conferred under any law of the United States,

children.heading: Relationship of Agreement to State law
children.label: b Relationship of Agreement to State law

children.heading: Legal challenge
children.label: 1 Legal challenge
children.text: No State law, or the application thereof, may be declared invalid as to any person or circumstance on the ground that the provision or application is inconsistent with the Agreement, except in an action brought by the United States for the purpose of declaring such law or application invalid.

children.label: A
children.text: any law of a political subdivision of a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: any State law regulating or taxing the business of insurance.

children.heading: Effect of Agreement with respect to private remedies
children.label: c Effect of Agreement with respect to private remedies

children.text: shall have any cause of action or defense under the Agreement or by virtue of congressional approval thereof; or

may challenge, in any action brought under any provision of law, any action or inaction by any department, agency, or other instrumentality of the United States, any State, or any political subdivision of a State, on the ground that such action or inaction is inconsistent with the Agreement.
